title: App Download Stalled
description: Detects when app downloads appear to have stalled, based on failed download progress events. DISABLED: the V2 EnrollmentTerminationHandler emits a synthetic download_progress with status=failed when it promotes Installing -> Error on terminal ESP-Apps failure. That synthetic event fires AFTER the download already completed (e.g. session 080edee9 saw download_progress 100% at 10:34:30 then download_progress failed at 10:41:08), so 'status=failed' alone is a false signal. The engine's `suppressByEvent` cannot filter by a third event's `status` field, so we can't directly say 'suppress when a prior download_progress for the same appId reached 100%' — re-enable only after the engine grows a status-aware suppression filter OR after we introduce a dedicated `download_stalled` event type that doesn't share its eventType with the post-mortem synthesis path.
explanation: Download progress events show a failed download, and at least one app installation also failed. This pattern suggests content delivery issues preventing apps from being downloaded successfully.

remediation.title: Investigate download issues
steps: Check network connectivity and bandwidth during the enrollment
Check if a proxy or firewall is blocking content delivery
Review Delivery Optimization settings and peer availability
